message:
test: add long-lived workspace stress suite

beads_rust-rsei.3.4

files.patch:
@@ -544,7 +544,7 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","title":"Add a crash-injection matrix for all critical mutation and sync phases","description":"## Purpose
Add crash-injection coverage across the critical mutation and sync phases.

## Required Operations
- create
- update
- close/reopen/defer/undefer
- delete
- label/comment mutation
- sync import/export/rebuild/recovery paths

## Required Crash Points
- before primary write
- after primary write but before derived-state rebuild
- during temp-file write
- before/after rename
- before dirty-flag clear
- during/after backup/quarantine creation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each crash point has explicit expected post-restart behavior.
- No crash leaves the workspace silently misclassified as healthy.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:35.916711828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:58.412355354Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crash-consistency","reliability","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:49.444077784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:58.411766251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:35.916711828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":197,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Future-self note: this bead is about interruption semantics, not throughput. Prefer fewer operations with well-defined crash points over broad but shallow coverage.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","title":"Broaden multi-process and mixed-agent contention scenarios","description":"## Purpose
Expand concurrency coverage from narrow lock-timeout scenarios to realistic multi-process and mixed-agent usage.

## Cover
- concurrent writers on one workspace
- read/write races across commands
- multiple agents issuing different command families interleaved
- routed/external workspace access during local mutation
- background processes touching the same `.beads` directory

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests define which outcomes are acceptable vs unacceptable.
- Contention results in bounded failure/retry behavior, not drift or corruption.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:37.773727464Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T11:38:47.309252200Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T11:31:29.228335520Z","close_reason":"Expanded bounded-failure concurrency coverage and validated integrity invariants","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agents","concurrency","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:09.543615058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:37.773727464Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":198,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Important consideration: success here does not mean every race magically succeeds. It means failures are explicit, bounded, and non-corrupting.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:39Z"},{"id":218,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Working this now in tests/e2e_concurrency.rs. Scope: add coverage for interleaved command families on one workspace, plus external BEADS_DIR/status access against the same workspace during local mutation, with final integrity assertions to distinguish acceptable lock contention from corruption/drift.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:07:24Z"},{"id":219,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed in tests/e2e_concurrency.rs. Added env-clearing/env-aware helper coverage, broadened contention assertions to bounded explicit failures vs corruption, added interleaved-command-family and external-BEADS_DIR/background-status scenarios, and tightened existing concurrency cases to assert final integrity based on successful operations. Verification: rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_concurrency -- --nocapture, rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets, rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:31:44Z"},{"id":221,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added broader contention coverage in tests/e2e_concurrency.rs for mixed command families, routed/external access, background sync status checks, and actor-oriented mutation scenarios. Also stabilized e2e_concurrent_writes_succeed_with_retry with an explicit lock timeout for deterministic remote verification. Verified with rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_concurrency -- --nocapture, rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_concurrency e2e_concurrent_writes_succeed_with_retry -- --nocapture, rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets, and cargo fmt --check.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:38:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","title":"Model filesystem realism and external interference scenarios","description":"## Purpose
Model filesystem and storage anomalies that hermetic tempdir tests almost never hit.

## Cover
- sidecar mismatch and disappearance
- rename failure / interrupted atomic export
- disk-full or temp-file allocation failure
- permissions changes
- external sync/editor/cloud-drive interference on `.beads`
- unusual HOME/path/env interactions that affect discovery and storage location

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests assert data preservation and honest diagnostics.
- We understand which anomalies are tolerated, which trigger repair, and which require operator intervention.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:39.716569260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:10:43.566934395Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["filesystem","ops","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:19.121325297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:39.716569260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":199,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This is where lab conditions diverge sharply from the field. If a scenario only happens on a user's real filesystem, the current tempdir-first suite is likely blind to it.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:41Z"},{"id":228,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Workspace-path audit progress: fixed  redirect provenance, fixed  so missing explicit metadata JSONL no longer falls back to discovered legacy JSONL, fixed  so configured external JSONL paths are validated with the external-path policy instead of being flagged as outside .beads, and strengthened workspace replay assertions to use resolved database/custom metadata paths. Verification is currently limited to rustfmt/git-diff checks and targeted reasoning because the rch worker fleet is unreachable right now and I am avoiding local cargo fallback per repo rules.","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:37:53Z"},{"id":229,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Correction to previous note: fixed where redirect provenance; fixed doctor so a missing explicit metadata JSONL path no longer falls back to a discovered legacy JSONL; fixed doctor so a configured external JSONL path is validated with the external-path policy instead of being flagged as outside .beads; and strengthened workspace replay assertions to use the resolved database/custom metadata paths. Verification is currently limited to rustfmt/git-diff checks and targeted reasoning because the rch worker fleet is unreachable right now and I am avoiding local cargo fallback per repo rules.","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:38:01Z"},{"id":230,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","author":"IcyCitadel","text":"Progress update: audited workspace discovery, where/info/doctor path reporting, and workspace failure replay coverage. Fixed redirect provenance in `where`, fixed `doctor` so a missing explicitly configured metadata JSONL path is surfaced instead of silently falling back to a discovered legacy file, corrected the config unit test for external DB override workspace fallback and added the missing no-workspace error case, and updated workspace replay assertions to validate the resolved database path from `where --json` for custom metadata DB paths. Verification: rustfmt --check passed on touched files; git diff --check passed; rch remote tests passed for discover_beads_dir_with_cli_from_* filters, resolve_where_output_preserves_redirect_origin, and test_collect_doctor_report_surfaces_missing_explicit_metadata_jsonl.","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:49:47Z"},{"id":231,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.3","author":"IcyCitadel","text":"Follow-up: full rch cargo check --all-targets did not expose new code errors in these touched paths, but it failed remotely on vmi1227854 with No space left on device while compiling unrelated integration-test targets. A narrower rch cargo check --lib still surfaced one local warning in doctor.rs (unused std::fs::self import); I removed that import locally afterward.","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:03:34Z"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","title":"Add a long-running single-workspace stress suite","description":"## Purpose
Create a long-running stress suite that reuses one workspace across many commands and processes to surface drift, stale assumptions, and accumulated-state bugs.

## Cover
- repeated issue lifecycle operations
- repeated sync/import/export status checks
- intermittent failures and restarts
- mixed human-style and agent-style command ordering

## Acceptance Criteria
- The suite can run for a meaningful duration without manual babysitting.
- Failures leave behind enough artifacts to classify and reproduce.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:41.731866221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:37.660222051Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["long-lived","reliability","stress"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:28.600633433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:41.731866221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:37.659700575Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":200,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead exists because the product is a stateful CLI, not a stateless request handler. Bugs that require dozens or hundreds of operations on the same workspace will never show up in per-test fresh-init patterns.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:43Z"}]}
+{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","title":"Add a long-running single-workspace stress suite","description":"## Purpose
Create a long-running stress suite that reuses one workspace across many commands and processes to surface drift, stale assumptions, and accumulated-state bugs.

## Cover
- repeated issue lifecycle operations
- repeated sync/import/export status checks
- intermittent failures and restarts
- mixed human-style and agent-style command ordering

## Acceptance Criteria
- The suite can run for a meaningful duration without manual babysitting.
- Failures leave behind enough artifacts to classify and reproduce.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:41.731866221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:35:25.309497352Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:35:25.309127179Z","close_reason":"Added deterministic long-lived single-workspace stress suite with mixed create/update/label/comment/close/reopen/sync/read/expected-failure flow; verified targeted stress test, workspace evolution tests, fmt, check, clippy, and ubs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["long-lived","reliability","stress"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:28.600633433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:41.731866221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:37.659700575Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":200,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead exists because the product is a stateful CLI, not a stateless request handler. Bugs that require dozens or hundreds of operations on the same workspace will never show up in per-test fresh-init patterns.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","title":"Harden detection, recovery, and no-data-loss guarantees","description":"## Why
Even with better tests, the product must behave safely when it encounters a damaged or inconsistent workspace in production. Detection, recovery, and operator messaging need to be consistent and trustworthy.

## Scope
Unify anomaly classification, preserve evidence before repair, verify post-recovery state, and make `doctor`/startup/write behavior agree.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Automatic recovery is explicit, reversible, and validated.
- We do not report "OK" for workspaces that are only partially readable or that fail on writes.
- Recovery logic never silently hides potential data loss.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:43.783459850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:09.162394901Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor","recovery","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:43.783459850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:59.944386891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:09.161785269Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":201,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This track is where field pain becomes product behavior. The product must either operate safely, recover safely, or stop with actionable evidence; the current gray zone in between is unacceptable.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","title":"Unify anomalous workspace classification across startup, doctor, and write paths","description":"## Purpose
Create one canonical anomaly classifier that every entry point uses when evaluating a workspace: startup/open, explicit `doctor`, mutation paths, sync, and recovery logic.

## Scope
- define shared anomaly classes for malformed SQLite pages, WAL/SHM mismatch, JSONL parse drift, metadata contradictions, stale dirty/export state, and broken derived caches
- remove ad hoc command-specific interpretations of the same evidence
- ensure structured errors and operator-facing diagnostics use the same language

## Acceptance Criteria
- A given workspace snapshot receives the same classification regardless of which command hits it first.
- Recovery and write decisions are driven by named health states rather than scattered heuristics.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:49:21.619173305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:21.789181630Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["classification","diagnostics","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:47.622950600Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:57.607408234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:49:21.619173305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":203,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead is the hinge point for the whole hardening track. If startup says “healthy enough” while writes or sync say “catastrophic corruption,” the user experiences chaos even when each individual code path looks locally reasonable.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","title":"Quarantine corrupt DB artifacts before automatic rebuild or repair","description":"## Purpose
When corruption or unsafe drift is detected, preserve the original DB artifacts before any rebuild, retry, or repair action mutates evidence.

## Scope
- move or snapshot suspect SQLite artifacts and sidecars into a clearly labeled quarantine area
- record why quarantine happened and what command triggered it
- make rebuild-from-JSONL and similar repair flows operate on fresh targets rather than on top of ambiguous debris

## Acceptance Criteria
- Recovery never silently overwrites the only copy of a damaged DB or WAL.
- Operators can inspect and attach quarantined artifacts to incident reports.
- Automatic repair paths become auditable and reversible.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:50:44.050503419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:24.853710150Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["quarantine","recovery","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:50:44.050503419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:06.949746141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":204,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"The root reliability principle here is evidence preservation. A “successful” automatic rebuild that destroys the original failing artifacts is still a product failure because it erases the operator’s ability to diagnose, prove, or safely roll back what happened.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:24Z"}]}

@@ -3085,11 +3085,151 @@ pub mod catalog {
             ])
     }
 
+    /// Deterministic single-workspace stress story that repeats mixed human/agent command ordering.
+    #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]
+    pub fn long_lived_mixed_order_stress(seed: u64, iterations: usize) -> WorkspaceEvolutionPlan {
+        let iterations = iterations.max(1);
+        let mut steps = Vec::with_capacity(iterations.saturating_mul(12).saturating_add(8));
+
+        steps.push(captured_create_step(
+            seed,
+            "stress-anchor",
+            "anchor",
+            "create_anchor",
+        ));
+        steps.push(sync_flush_step("flush_anchor"));
+
+        for iteration in 0..iterations {
+            let iteration_seed = seed
+                .checked_add(u64::try_from(iteration).expect("iteration should fit into u64"))
+                .expect("stress seed should not overflow");
+            let alias = format!("stress_{iteration}");
+            let create_label = format!("create_stress_{iteration}");
+            steps.push(captured_create_step(
+                iteration_seed,
+                &format!("stress-{iteration}"),
+                &alias,
+                &create_label,
+            ));
+
+            steps.push(command_step(
+                vec![
+                    "sync".to_string(),
+                    "--status".to_string(),
+                    "--json".to_string(),
+                ],
+                &format!("status_after_create_{iteration}"),
+            ));
+            steps.push(stale_read_step("list", &format!("stale_list_{iteration}")));
+            steps.push(command_step(
+                vec![
+                    "update".to_string(),
+                    format!("{{{alias}}}"),
+                    "--status".to_string(),
+                    "in_progress".to_string(),
+                    "--json".to_string(),
+                ],
+                &format!("agent_claim_{iteration}"),
+            ));
+            steps.push(command_step(
+                vec![
+                    "label".to_string(),
+                    "add".to_string(),
+                    format!("{{{alias}}}"),
+                    format!("stress-{iteration}"),
+                    "--json".to_string(),
+                ],
+                &format!("human_label_{iteration}"),
+            ));
+            steps.push(command_step(
+                vec![
+                    "comments".to_string(),
+                    "add".to_string(),
+                    format!("{{{alias}}}"),
+                    seeded_reason(iteration_seed, "stress-note"),
+                    "--json".to_string(),
+                ],
+                &format!("agent_comment_{iteration}"),
+            ));
+
+            if iteration % 3 == 1 {
+                steps.push(close_step(
+                    iteration_seed,
+                    &alias,
+                    "stress-close",
+                    &format!("close_stress_{iteration}"),
+                    true,
+                ));
+                steps.push(reopen_step(
+                    iteration_seed,
+                    &alias,
+                    "stress-reopen",
+                    &format!("reopen_stress_{iteration}"),
+                ));
+            }
+
+            if iteration % 4 == 2 {
+                steps.push(WorkspaceEvolutionStep::command(
+                    WorkspaceEvolutionCommand::new(
+                        ScenarioCommand::new(["show", "missing-stress-id", "--json"])
+                            .with_label(format!("expected_missing_show_{iteration}")),
+                    )
+                    .expect_failure(),
+                ));
+            }
+
+            if iteration % 2 == 0 {
+                steps.push(sync_flush_step(&format!("flush_even_{iteration}")));
+            } else {
+                steps.push(command_step(
+                    vec![
+                        "sync".to_string(),
+                        "--import-only".to_string(),
+                        "--force".to_string(),
+                        "--json".to_string(),
+                    ],
+                    &format!("import_odd_{iteration}"),
+                ));
+            }
+
+            if iteration % 5 == 4 {
+                steps.push(WorkspaceEvolutionStep::snapshot(format!(
+                    "checkpoint_{iteration}"
+                )));
+            }
+        }
+
+        steps.push(command_step(
+            vec!["ready".to_string(), "--json".to_string()],
+            "ready_after_stress",
+        ));
+        steps.push(command_step(
+            vec!["info".to_string(), "--json".to_string()],
+            "info_after_stress",
+        ));
+        steps.push(command_step(
+            vec!["where".to_string(), "--json".to_string()],
+            "where_after_stress",
+        ));
+        steps.push(sync_flush_step("flush_after_stress"));
+        steps.push(command_step(
+            vec!["doctor".to_string(), "--json".to_string()],
+            "doctor_after_stress",
+        ));
+
+        WorkspaceEvolutionPlan::new("long_lived_mixed_order_stress", seed)
+            .with_description(
+                "Seeded long-running single-workspace stress story covering repeated issue lifecycle operations, sync status/import/export checks, expected failures, process restarts, and mixed human/agent command ordering.",
+            )
+            .with_steps(steps)
+    }
+
     /// Deterministic catalog of long-lived workspace evolutions for a seed.
     pub fn long_lived_seed_suite(seed: u64) -> Vec<WorkspaceEvolutionPlan> {
         vec![
             long_lived_dependency_story(seed),
             long_lived_recovery_story(seed),
+            long_lived_mixed_order_stress(seed, 6),
         ]
     }
 }
